title: Trisetum phleoides
intro: Trisetum phleoides, also known as Phleoid grass, is a type of perennial grass native to Europe and parts of Asia. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to promote digestive health and relieve symptoms of indigestion, bloating, and gas, due to its carminative properties. The bioactive constituents of Trisetum phleoides include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant are infusions, decoctions, and teas, often used to treat digestive issues and promote overall well-being.
taxonomy: Trisetum phleoides is a species of grass in the family Poaceae. It belongs to the tribe Poeae and is part of the subfamily Pooideae. The genus Trisetum is classified under the family Poaceae, which includes various other types of grasses. Trisetum phleoides is native to certain regions and is often found in grasslands and open woodlands. This species is a type of annual or perennial grass that grows in specific habitats.
